Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Soluble GA receptor determining GA perception specificity; required for GA homeostatic regulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GID1 directly binds bioactive GAs. 2. GID1 forms a GA-dependent complex with DELLA proteins. 3. Central GA signaling components including GID1 are required for transcriptional homeostasis of GA metabolism genes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: binding assay; yeast complex formation; mutant analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rice GID1 prefers GA4 over GA1 in binding/response assays, although GA1 is major bioactive GA in vegetative rice tissues.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repress GA responses and participate in homeostatic control of GA metabolism genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DELLA proteins repress GA signaling and are degraded upon interaction with GID1-GA complex. 2. DELLA proteins are required for normal feedback regulation of GA metabolism genes. 3. Arabidopsis DELLA loss-of-function reduces AtGA3ox1 transcript levels even in a GA-deficient background.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast interaction; degradation assays; mutant analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RGA and GAI are specific DELLA genes discussed as PIL5 direct targets.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required signaling F-box proteins for GA homeostatic regulation; loss elevates GA through altered feedback
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SLY1/GID2 are central GA signaling components required for homeostatic gene regulation. 2. In rice gid2 mutants, OsGA20ox2 is upregulated and GA1 levels become highly elevated.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; transcript analysis; hormone measurement

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Rice DELLA repressor expressed in elongating/dividing tissues and aleurone, marking GA response sites
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: rapidly elongating or dividing tissues; aleurone
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SLR1 expression overlaps with active growth tissues in rice. 2. Aleurone expresses SLR1 but not GA biosynthetic genes, supporting nonproducing but GA-responsive tissue status.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents tissue-specific GA response rather than metabolism enzyme function.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early GA biosynthesis from GGDP to ent-copalyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GGDP to ent-copalyl diphosphate as the first committed step of GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for bioactive GA biosynthesis; loss causes severe GA-deficient dwarfism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPS catalyzes the first committed step from GGDP to ent-copalyl diphosphate. 2. CPS is plastid localized. 3. In Arabidopsis, null alleles cause severe GA-deficient dwarfism. 4. Overexpression increases ent-kaurene, ent-kaurenoic acid, and GA12 but not bioactive GAs, indicating early steps are not the primary control point.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; overexpression; metabolite profiling; subcellular localization with GFP fusion
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis CPS is encoded by GA1. Rice has two functional CPS genes, but only one likely functions in GA biosynthesis; others can function in phytoalexin-related diterpene pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormonal control of plant growth and development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed mainly in rapidly growing tissues in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited alone for increasing bioactive GA because overexpression raises early intermediates without increasing active GAs

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early GA biosynthesis from ent-copalyl diphosphate to ent-kaurene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for GA biosynthesis; null mutants are severe GA-deficient dwarfs in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KS catalyzes formation of ent-kaurene from ent-copalyl diphosphate. 2. KS is plastid localized. 3. Genetic studies indicate a single GA-biosynthetic KS gene in rice despite related genomic sequences.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Arabidopsis KS corresponds to GA2 mutant locus context in review.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormonal control of growth and development

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidation steps from ent-kaurene to ent-kaurenoic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential oxidation at C-19 of ent-kaurene to produce ent-kaurenoic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for GA biosynthesis; null mutants cause severe GA-deficient dwarfism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KO oxidizes ent-kaurene at C-19 to ent-kaurenoic acid. 2. KO is localized to the outer plastid membrane. 3. KO is encoded by a single GA-biosynthetic gene in Arabidopsis and likely also in rice despite KO-like sequences.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtKO promoter is bound by tobacco regulator RSG in heterologous assays.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidation steps from ent-kaurenoic acid to GA12
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for production of GA12, the common precursor of bioactive GAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KAO converts ent-kaurenoic acid to GA12. 2. KAO localizes to the endoplasmic reticulum. 3. It provides the central precursor for later bioactive GA synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: C20-GAs including GA12 and GA53 pathway intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential oxidation at C-20, including loss of C-20 as CO2 and formation of gamma-lactone, converting C20-GAs to C19-GAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes bioactive GA production; increased expression raises GA levels and causes GA-overdose phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA20ox generates C19 precursors for bioactive GAs. 2. GA20ox family members are major regulatory nodes and are differentially controlled by developmental and environmental cues. 3. Overexpression increases GA levels and produces GA-overdose phenotypes. 4. GA20ox genes are frequent targets of feedback, hormonal, and photoperiodic regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; transcript analysis; mutant analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA feedback; auxin; photoperiod; light; ABA-related seed development cues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major target for manipulating plant stature and growth through GA content
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Includes AtGA20ox1, OsGA20ox1, OsGA20ox2/SD1, PsGA20ox1, SoGA20ox1, LtGA20ox1, LpGA20ox1 discussed in species-specific contexts.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: GA4, GA1, and related bioactive gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: inactive precursors such as GA9 and GA20
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: bioactive GAs such as GA4 and GA1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a 3β-hydroxyl group into inactive GA precursors to generate bioactive GAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly activates bioactive GAs; essential determinant of active GA levels in many tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA3ox catalyzes the final activation step to bioactive GAs. 2. GA3ox genes are encoded by multigene families with developmental and environmental specialization. 3. AtGA3ox1 and AtGA3ox2 have overlapping but distinct roles in vegetative development. 4. Some GA3ox enzymes also have minor side activity producing GA3/GA6 or weak GA13ox activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: reverse genetics; enzyme assay; transcript analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light; cold; auxin; ABA; feedback regulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TaGA3ox2 from wheat shows weak GA13ox activity in vitro. OsGA3ox2 is essential for α-amylase induction in germinating rice grains; OsGA3ox1 is not sufficient. LsGA3ox1 and PsGA3ox1 are prominent environmentally regulated examples.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates GA12 at C-13 to generate GA53, precursor of GA1 in the 13-hydroxylated pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Would determine flux into the GA1-producing 13-hydroxylated pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA13ox activity converts GA12 to GA53 for the GA1 branch. 2. Genes encoding bona fide GA13ox had not yet been identified in plants at the time of this review. 3. Wheat TaGA3ox2 shows only weak in vitro GA13ox side activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: biochemical inference; in vitro activity of TaGA3ox2 side reaction
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a major summarized missing enzyme in the review, but the corresponding plant gene remained unidentified.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Bioactive gibberellins and GA precursor pools
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: C19-GAs or C20-GAs depending on class
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 2β-hydroxylation of bioactive GAs or their precursors; class I/II enzymes use C19-GAs and class III enzymes use C20-GAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces bioactive GA levels by deactivating active GAs or depleting precursor pools
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA2ox enzymes are the best-characterized GA deactivation enzymes. 2. Class I and II act on C19-GAs, whereas class III acts on C20-GAs to deplete precursor pools. 3. GA2ox genes are major targets of developmental, hormonal, light, cold, and stress regulation. 4. Upregulation of GA2ox lowers GA levels and growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: enzyme assays; mutant analysis; transcript analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA treatment; light; cold; auxin; salt stress; meristem regulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to reduce plant stature or stress-associated growth through enhanced GA deactivation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific genes highlighted include AtGA2ox1, AtGA2ox2, AtGA2ox4, AtGA2ox6, AtGA2ox7, OsGA2ox1, OsGA2ox3, PsGA2ox1, PsGA2ox2, and LsGA2ox2.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: GA4; GA9; GA12 and related non-13-hydroxylated GAs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epoxidizes the 16,17-double bond of non-13-hydroxylated GAs, leading to deactivated products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major deactivation enzyme in rice upper internodes; lowers GA levels and restrains internode elongation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: principal deactivation enzyme in upper internodes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EUI/CYP714D1 defines a new GA deactivation mechanism via 16α,17-epoxidation. 2. eui mutants accumulate extremely high bioactive GA levels. 3. Overexpression produces 16α,17-dihydrodiols and GA-deficiency phenotypes. 4. The mechanism may be broadly relevant across plants.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Petunia hybrida
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: various GAs including bioactive GAs and precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates the C-6 carboxyl group of GAs using S-adenosyl-L-methionine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deactivates GAs; ectopic expression causes dwarfism and GA deficiency; loss elevates seed GA levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAMT1 methylates multiple GAs to GA methyl esters. 2. Ectopic expression in Arabidopsis, tobacco, and petunia causes dwarfism. 3. In Arabidopsis seeds, GAMT1 contributes to GA deactivation during development and germination.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; ectopic expression; double-mutant analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: predominantly in developing and germinating seeds

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana tabacum; Petunia hybrida
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: various GAs including bioactive GAs and precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates the C-6 carboxyl group of GAs using S-adenosyl-L-methionine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deactivates GAs; ectopic expression causes dwarfism and GA deficiency; loss elevates seed GA levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAMT2 functions with GAMT1 in GA methylation. 2. Ectopic expression causes GA-deficiency phenotypes in several species. 3. gamt1 gamt2 double mutants accumulate more bioactive GAs in developing seeds and show increased resistance to a GA biosynthesis inhibitor during germination.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; ectopic expression; double-mutant analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: predominantly in developing and germinating seeds

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression of GA20ox and indirect activation of GA2ox via cytokinin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GA20ox genes; GA2ox genes indirectly/directly depending on member
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses GA20ox expression and promotes low-GA state in the shoot apical meristem
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major developmental controller establishing low-GA regime in meristems
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces endogenous GA levels, promoting meristem identity and indeterminacy
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ectopic expression; direct DNA binding; expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KNOX proteins repress GA biosynthesis in shoot meristems. 2. They suppress GA20ox expression, excluding GA biosynthesis from meristem corpus. 3. They also help establish a low-GA regime together with cytokinin pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes a regulator family rather than one gene; NTH15 and STM are concrete examples.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly suppresses GA20ox by binding its first intron
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct meristem-associated suppressor of GA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lowers endogenous GA levels and contributes to dwarfism/meristem identity maintenance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: direct binding assay; ectopic expression; endogenous GA measurement
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NTH15 directly binds the first intron of GA20ox. 2. This suppresses GA biosynthesis in meristem tissue. 3. KNOX-mediated GA suppression helps maintain indeterminate cells.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates AtGA2ox4 and AtGA2ox6 at the base of the meristem
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: contributes to establishment of low-GA meristem environment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes low GA levels in shoot meristem to maintain meristem identity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis; genetic studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STM promotes expression of GA deactivation genes AtGA2ox4 and AtGA2ox6. 2. This contributes to a low-GA regime in the meristem. 3. STM acts with cytokinin-related pathways in meristem maintenance.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: promotes increase in GA levels during floral meristem differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Raises GA biosynthesis to promote shift from meristem identity to floral organ differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microarray; promoter binding in vitro and in vivo
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AG elevates AtGA3ox1 expression. 2. AG directly binds the AtGA3ox1 promoter. 3. Increased GA likely promotes floral meristem differentiation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Connects floral homeotic regulation with GA metabolism.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Prevents ectopic activation of AtGA3ox2 in immature seeds
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: seed maturation regulator maintaining low GA state
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses GA accumulation in immature seeds
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lec2 mutants show elevated bioactive GA in immature seeds. 2. This is associated with ectopic AtGA3ox2 expression. 3. LEC2 is therefore a negative regulator of GA accumulation during seed maturation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct promoter binding not stated for LEC2 in this review.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression of GA biosynthesis and positive regulation of ABA
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses AtGA3ox2 directly and represses AtGA3ox1 and AtGA20ox1 upon induction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key coordinator balancing GA and ABA during seed maturation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lowers bioactive GA levels in immature seeds while increasing ABA
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: FUS3 protein is positively regulated by ABA and negatively by GA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; transient induction; direct DNA binding in vitro
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fus3 mutants have elevated GA levels due in part to ectopic AtGA3ox2 expression. 2. FUS3 directly binds RY elements in the AtGA3ox2 promoter. 3. FUS3 represses GA biosynthesis genes and promotes ABA accumulation, establishing GA-ABA balance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A central hormonal balance regulator in seeds.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lowers GA content during embryogenesis via increased deactivation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGA2ox6 is a direct in vivo target of AGL15. 2. AGL15 overexpression activates AtGA2ox6 in seeds. 3. AGL15 likely lowers GA levels during embryogenesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; GA-dependent negative modulation via 14-3-3 sequestration
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: AtKO promoter-binding activity reported; GA20ox feedback regulation implicated
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates KO-related expression and is required for normal feedback regulation of GA20ox
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: homeostatic activator linking GA status to biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports GA biosynthesis; dominant-negative RSG represses KO expression and causes dwarfism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dominant-negative transgenics; promoter binding; localization studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RSG binds the AtKO promoter. 2. Dominant-negative RSG represses KO expression and causes dwarfism. 3. GA negatively modulates RSG function by promoting 14-3-3-mediated cytoplasmic sequestration. 4. RSG may participate in feedback regulation of GA20ox.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role appears restricted to GA20ox and not GA3ox feedback.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Mediates GA negative feedback response at AtGA3ox1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: candidate mediator for gene-specific GA homeostatic control
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Impacts feedback regulation of AtGA3ox1 and thereby GA biosynthesis homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AGF1 binds a 43-bp cis-element mediating GA negative feedback in the AtGA3ox1 promoter. 2. The mechanism linking GA signal to AGF1 remains unclear. 3. GA3ox1 and GA20ox1 feedback may use distinct mechanisms.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GA does not alter AGF1 transcript or nuclear localization according to the review.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression of biosynthesis and activation of deactivation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: mediator of DELLA-dependent feedback inhibition in rice
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Decreases GA1 levels and causes semidwarf phenotype when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; cosuppression; promoter binding in vitro; hormone measurement
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsYAB1 expression depends on DELLA. 2. Overexpression reduces GA1 and causes semidwarfism. 3. It downregulates OsGA3ox2 and upregulates OsGA2ox3. 4. Cosuppression impairs GA-dependent repression of OsGA3ox2.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Binds a GA-responsive element in the OsGA3ox2 promoter in vitro.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: light-regulated seed GA metabolism and GA responsiveness
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: represses GA3ox expression and activates GA2ox expression in darkness; degraded upon phytochrome activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses AtGA3ox1/2 and activates AtGA2ox2 indirectly; directly binds DELLA gene promoters RGA and GAI
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key light-labile repressor controlling germination-associated GA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Keeps GA4 low in dark-imbibed seeds and suppresses germination; light removes PIL5 to increase GA4
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP; gene expression analysis; light-response genetics
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIL5 is a negative regulator of phytochrome-dependent seed germination. 2. In darkness it suppresses GA3ox and promotes GA2ox expression, lowering GA4. 3. Light-activated phytochrome causes PIL5 degradation, allowing GA accumulation. 4. PIL5 also binds DELLA gene promoters and links GA metabolism with responsiveness.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: integrates light signaling with hormonal balance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not directly bind AtGA3ox1/2 or AtGA2ox2 promoters according to ChIP, implying intermediary factors.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: cold- and light-responsive seed GA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses AtGA3ox genes during control of seed germination responses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: light-stable repressor affecting cold stratification response
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restrains germination in part by limiting GA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPT is a light-stable repressor of seed germination. 2. It controls responses to cold stratification partly through AtGA3ox genes. 3. It also contributes somewhat to phytochrome-dependent light responses.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of GA deactivation target(s)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Targets AtGA2ox7, a C20-GA-specific GA2ox, to reduce bioactive GA levels under salt stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: salt-inducible regulator linking stress tolerance with low GA growth state
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces GA4 and causes dwarfism while increasing salt survival
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; stress assays; metabolite profiling; target identification
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DDF1 is induced strongly by high salinity. 2. Overexpression lowers GA4 and causes dwarfism. 3. DDF1-overexpressing plants survive salt stress better. 4. AtGA2ox7 is identified as a target, linking salt response to increased GA deactivation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Target assignment to AtGA2ox7 cited as unpublished data in the review.
